data.title: CFTC Enhances Efficiency with AI Amid Workforce Reduction
data.description:
The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is reportedly improving its monitoring and investigative capabilities through the integration of AI and automation technologies, despite a workforce reduction of about 25% since 2025. According to Odaily, CFTC Chairman Mike Selig confirmed that the agency is conducting multiple investigations into prediction markets to address issues such as insider trading, fraud, and market manipulation, emphasizing a zero-tolerance policy towards illegal market activities.

In addition, the Senate is advancing the Digital Asset Market Transparency Act, which aims to position the CFTC as the primary regulator for non-securities cryptocurrency trading, including assets like Bitcoin and Ethereum.

data.title: ECB's Villeroy: Central Bank Independence Leads to Lower Inflation and Interest Rates
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member François Villeroy de Galhau has emphasized the benefits of central bank independence. According to Jin10, Villeroy stated that historical evidence shows that when central banks operate independently, it results in lower inflation and interest rates. This assertion underscores the importance of maintaining the autonomy of central banks to ensure economic stability and effective monetary policy. Villeroy's comments come amid ongoing discussions about the role and influence of central banks in managing economic conditions.

data.title: Charles Hoskinson Warns of Potential Bitcoin Freeze with BIP-361 Proposal
data.description: Charles Hoskinson has raised concerns about the proposed Bitcoin Improvement Proposal BIP-361, suggesting it could lead to the permanent freezing of approximately 1.7 million BTC if implemented. According to NS3.AI, Hoskinson argued that the proposal is being incorrectly categorized as a soft fork. He further explained that early-mined coins would not be able to migrate under this proposal, potentially impacting a significant portion of Bitcoin's supply.

data.title: AI TRENDS | IMF Chief Warns Against AI Revolution Repeating Globalization Mistakes
data.description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva has cautioned that the artificial intelligence revolution should avoid repeating the pitfalls of globalization, where many individuals who lost their jobs never reintegrated into the economic system. According to Jin10, Georgieva emphasized the importance of ensuring that the benefits of AI are widely shared and that measures are in place to support those displaced by technological advancements. She highlighted the need for policies that promote inclusivity and provide opportunities for reskilling and upskilling to prevent long-term economic exclusion. Georgieva's remarks underscore the challenges and responsibilities that come with the rapid development and implementation of AI technologies.

data.title: CFTC Chairman: AI has helped address staff reductions and conduct numerous prediction market surveys.
data.description: Mike Selig, chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), stated that although the CFTC's staff has shrunk by about a quarter since 2025, the agency is operating more efficiently in monitoring and investigations by introducing AI and automation technologies. Selig confirmed that the CFTC is conducting multiple investigations into prediction markets to address insider trading, fraud, and market manipulation, and stated that the agency has zero tolerance for illegal market activities. Furthermore, the Senate's proposed digital asset market transparency bill aims to place the CFTC in a central regulatory position over non-securities cryptocurrency trading, covering assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum (ETH).

data.title: Gulf Arab and European Leaders Anticipate Six-Month Timeline for US-Iran Peace Deal
data.description: Some Gulf Arab and European leaders are projecting that a peace agreement between the United States and Iran could take approximately six months to finalize. Bloomberg posted on X, citing officials familiar with the situation, that these leaders are advocating for an extension of the current ceasefire to accommodate this anticipated timeline. The discussions are ongoing, and the involved parties are working towards a resolution that could stabilize the region. The extension of the ceasefire is seen as a crucial step in maintaining peace while negotiations continue.

data.title: Tempo Introduces Zones for Private Stablecoin Transactions
data.description: Tempo has unveiled a new feature called Zones, designed to enable enterprises to conduct private stablecoin transactions on parallel blockchains linked to its mainnet. According to NS3.AI, this innovation targets applications in payroll, treasury, settlement, and tokenized deposits. Tempo plans to roll out broader production deployments in phases.

data.title: Kenya Considers Domestic Gold Purchase Program to Diversify Forex Reserves
data.description: Kenya is exploring the possibility of launching a domestic gold purchase program as part of its strategy to diversify foreign exchange reserves, according to the Central Bank of Kenya Governor. This initiative aims to strengthen the country's economic stability by reducing reliance on traditional foreign currencies. According to Jin10, the move comes amid global economic uncertainties and fluctuating currency values, prompting nations to seek alternative reserve assets. The Central Bank of Kenya is evaluating the potential benefits and challenges of implementing such a program, which could enhance the country's financial resilience. The plan reflects a broader trend among nations to diversify their reserve holdings in response to shifting global economic dynamics.

data.title: U.S. Rail Diesel Demand Rises Amid Iran Conflict Disruptions
data.description: Demand for shipping diesel by rail within the United States surged in March, reflecting the impact of the ongoing conflict in Iran on global trading patterns. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how the disruptions have led to a scramble for this essential fuel. The war has significantly affected the availability and distribution of diesel, prompting increased reliance on rail transport to meet domestic needs. As the situation in Iran continues to unfold, the global energy market remains on edge, with countries adjusting their strategies to secure vital resources.

data.title: U.S. House Narrowly Rejects Bill to Limit Military Action Against Iran
data.description:
The U.S. House of Representatives narrowly voted against a Democratic-led bill on April 16, with a result of 214 in favor and 213 against. According to Odaily, the bill aimed to require U.S. President Donald Trump to cease military actions against Iran and seek congressional approval before any further military engagement. The Senate had already rejected the proposal the previous day.

Democrats have expressed their intention to continue introducing similar measures until either the conflict with Iran concludes or Congress authorizes further military action. Under the U.S. Constitution, only Congress has the power to declare war. A 1973 law stipulates that the President can only engage in limited military actions without congressional approval in response to attacks on the U.S. or imminent threats.

data.title: France Seeks to Calm Crypto Sector Amid Security Concerns
data.description:
French authorities are working to alleviate concerns among digital-asset executives and investors following a series of kidnappings and attempted abductions that pose a threat to the nation's aspirations of becoming a leading crypto hub. According to Bloomberg, these incidents have raised alarm within the cryptocurrency community, prompting officials to take action to ensure the safety and security of those involved in the sector.

The recent wave of criminal activities targeting individuals in the digital-asset industry has sparked fears that France's burgeoning reputation as a crypto-friendly environment could be undermined. The government is reportedly implementing measures to address these security challenges and reassure stakeholders of their commitment to maintaining a safe business environment. This situation highlights the delicate balance between fostering innovation in the rapidly evolving crypto space and ensuring the protection of those who operate within it. As France continues to position itself as a key player in the global cryptocurrency landscape, the effectiveness of its response to these security threats will be closely watched by industry participants and observers alike.

data.title: Solana Nears Resistance Zone with Potential for Future Gains
data.description: Solana (SOL) is currently trading close to the $84–$86 resistance zone. According to NS3.AI, CoinCodex analysts have projected that SOL could reach $103 by May 12, 2026. CoinGecko data indicates that SOL has increased by 2% over the past 24 hours, although it has experienced a decline of 10.4% over the previous month.

data.title: Gulf states and European officials believe it will take the United States six months to reach an agreement on Iran.
data.description: According to Bloomberg, regional officials familiar with the situation say some Gulf states and European leaders believe a peace agreement between the US and Iran could take about six months and advocate extending the current ceasefire to cover that period. These officials stated that leaders want the Strait of Hormuz to open immediately to restore energy flows and have privately warned of a potential global food crisis if the strait fails to reopen by next month. They also indicated that energy prices could rise further if the war continues longer. Officials said the Gulf states believe Iran still seeks to develop nuclear weapons, a view unchanged since the US and Israeli strikes against Iran, and therefore believe any peace agreement should prohibit Iran from uranium enrichment or possessing long-range ballistic missiles. However, the Gulf states generally oppose resuming fighting and hope the US will resolve its dispute with Iran through diplomatic channels. (Jinshi)

data.title: The bill limiting Trump's war powers was rejected by the US House of Representatives.
data.description: The U.S. House of Representatives narrowly rejected a Democratic-initiated bill on April 16, with 214 votes in favor and 213 against. The bill would have required President Trump to end military action against Iran and to obtain congressional authorization before taking further military action. The bill had already been rejected by the Senate the previous day. Democrats stated they would continue to introduce similar bills until the war with Iran ends or Congress authorizes further military action. Under the U.S. Constitution, only Congress can authorize war. A 1973 law stipulates that only in a national emergency arising from an attack on or imminent threat against the United States can the President take limited military action without congressional authorization. (Jin Shi)

data.title: The S&P 500 hit a record high of 7051 points.
data.description: The S&P 500 index has broken through the 7,000-point mark and once touched 7,051 points, setting a new all-time high.

data.title: Following Trump's announcement of a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, the Israeli military launched a large-scale airstrike on southern Lebanon.
data.description: It was learned on the evening of the 16th local time that after US President Trump announced a ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel at midnight Beirut time that day, the Israel Defense Forces launched a large-scale airstrike on several areas in southern Lebanon. Missile explosions could be heard in and around southern Lebanese cities, and the roar of warplanes continued. US President Trump said on the 16th that Lebanon and Israel would officially begin a 10-day ceasefire at 5 PM Eastern Time that day. (CCTV News)

data.title: ORDI Experiences Significant Liquidations in 24 Hours
data.description: According to Odaily, data from Coinglass reveals that ORDI experienced liquidations totaling approximately $30.29 million across the network in the past 24 hours, ranking third overall. Of this amount, long positions accounted for about $13.95 million, while short positions saw liquidations of approximately $16.33 million.

data.title: Zonda CEO Reveals Bitcoin Wallet Amid Controversy
data.description:
Zonda, a Polish cryptocurrency exchange, is facing scrutiny after CEO Przemysław Kral disclosed a cold wallet containing approximately 4,503 bitcoins. According to Foresight News, Kral revealed in a video statement on Thursday that the wallet, last active in November 2025, was supposed to be transferred by Zonda's founder and former CEO, Sylwester Suszek, who has been missing since March 2022. Kral denied allegations of fund misappropriation, stating that finding Suszek is crucial.

The disclosure comes amid multiple controversies surrounding Zonda, including investigations by Polish authorities and reports from blockchain analysis platform Recoveris suggesting potential insolvency due to a sharp decline in the exchange's hot wallet balance. Kral previously refuted bankruptcy claims on April 6, asserting that the platform holds over 4,500 bitcoins and remains solvent.

Kral noted that Zonda typically processes around 100,000 withdrawal requests annually, but received over 25,000 requests in a short period around April 6, attributing this surge to panic withdrawals triggered by negative media coverage.

He announced plans to take legal action against false accusations and assured users of the company's commitment to fulfilling withdrawal obligations. Polish lawmaker Tomasz Mentzen commented on X that Zonda might permanently lose access to the cold wallet due to Suszek's disappearance, though Kral did not confirm any loss of funds in his statement.

data.title: Mizuho: X Money's Potential to Disrupt U.S. Payment Industry Faces Regulatory Challenges
data.description: X Money, a financial feature launched by Elon Musk's X platform, has the potential to disrupt the U.S. payment industry, according to a report by Mizuho Bank research analysts. According to BlockBeats, analysts Dan Dolev and Andrew Jenkins highlighted in a client report that X Money is positioned as the financial infrastructure layer of the X platform, aiming to integrate instant messaging, banking deposits, and commercial transaction functions, similar to the 'super app' model of WeChat Pay or Alipay. With X platform's 500 to 600 million monthly active users and Musk's background as a co-founder of PayPal, X Money holds significant disruptive potential.However, analysts pointed out two major regulatory hurdles. Firstly, New York State's proposed CRYPTO Act, which aims to criminalize unlicensed virtual currency operations, could raise compliance barriers for X's future crypto integration plans. Secondly, the Clarity Act might limit non-bank financial platforms' ability to offer returns to users, potentially hindering X Money's plan to provide a 6% annualized return on cash balances, a product launch described as 'particularly sensitive' by the analysts.Mizuho also downgraded PayPal's (PYPL) stock rating to 'neutral,' noting that PayPal and its Venmo app face the most direct replacement risk as X targets the same peer-to-peer transfer and digital wallet market. This week, the X platform also introduced a new feature called 'Cashtags,' allowing users to view financial data on stocks and cryptocurrencies directly in their timeline.

data.title: ORDI's liquidation amount ranked third across all platforms, with approximately $20.96 million liquidated in 24 hours.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, ORDI saw approximately $20.96 million in liquidations across the entire network in the past 24 hours, ranking third. Of this, approximately $4.91 million were long positions liquidated, and approximately $16.05 million were short positions liquidated.
